mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, it increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns as valuations compress over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in UNRATE has a positive or zero rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, strong retail sales growth is interpreted by markets as inflationary, increasing the probability of further monetary tightening. This expectation of higher future rates outweighs the positive demand signal, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, the correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects robust economic activity and corporate output, which translates directly into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth, falling below a critical psychological threshold, signals an imminent economic slowdown. This triggers a rapid downward revision of earnings forecasts, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances following a PAYEMS growth print below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in housing starts serves as a leading indicator of weakening aggregate demand due to higher borrowing costs. This forward-looking signal leads to negative revisions in economic growth and earnings expectations, depressing equity prices.
failure_condition: When the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive, the correlation between the 3-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation remains elevated above the central bank's target, it implies a sustained period of restrictive monetary policy. This compresses equity multiples through a higher discount rate and expectations of slower economic growth, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive average 60-day forward return during periods when YoY CPI inflation is above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP signals a recessionary environment, leading to significant downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts. This broad-based pessimism drives negative equity market returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following the release of a negative quarterly GDP growth number, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.
